orb.approach: Build a single reusable `<Orb>` React component (client component) that mounts an r3f `<Canvas>` configured for low overhead, and wrap it in a smart loader `<MagicOrb>` that decides at runtime whether to render the WebGL orb or a CSS/canvas plasma-aura fallback. The WebGL orb is a refractive icosahedron sphere using drei's MeshTransmissionMaterial (glass/crystal refraction + chromatic aberration), with a smaller emissive inner "plasma core" sphere that bleeds blue light through the shell, a fresnel rim shader for the halo, drei Sparkles orbiting it, drei Float for idle bobbing, a drei Environment for realistic refraction reflections, and selective Bloom from @react-three/postprocessing for the glow. The whole canvas runs frameloop="demand" and is driven by a single shared store (Zustand-style or React context) so the SAME logical orb instance can be referenced and varied across every page rather than re-instantiated. Cursor parallax is handled by lerping a light position + slight rotation toward a normalized pointer vector inside useFrame, calling invalidate() so demand-mode still animates while interacting/visible. Mounting is gated behind an IntersectionObserver + visibility/reduced-motion/WebGL checks so it never blocks scroll, never paints offscreen, and always has a graceful fallback. Tie the orb's accent color to brand blue #6CBAD9 with #BFE0EE highlights so it reads as the genie's conjured crystal core from the logo.
libraries: @react-three/fiber@^8.17.10 (already in package.json — note: do NOT upgrade to v9 yet; v9 requires React 19 and the project is on React 18.3)
@react-three/drei@^9.114.3 (already in package.json — provides MeshTransmissionMaterial, Sparkles, Float, Environment, useTexture)
@react-three/postprocessing@^2.16.3 (MUST be added — Bloom/EffectComposer/Selection/Select live here, NOT in drei; pin to the 2.16.x line for r3f v8 + React 18 compatibility)
postprocessing@^6.36.4 (peer dep of @react-three/postprocessing — needed for KernelSize/Resolution enums)
framer-motion@^11.11.17 (already present — for fallback aura animation + reduced-motion useReducedMotion hook)

(optional, recommended) zustand@^4.5.5 — tiny shared store so the single orb's state/variant is global across pages without prop-drilling; the brief's stack doesn't list it but it's the pmndrs-canonical choice and ~1kb
techniques: Refractive crystal shell: drei <MeshTransmissionMaterial> on an icosahedronGeometry(1, 8-12) sphere. Key props: transmission={1}, thickness={1.2}, roughness={0.08}, ior={1.45}, chromaticAberration={0.06}, anisotropicBlur={0.4}, distortion={0.4}, distortionScale={0.4}, temporalDistortion={0.15}, samples={6} (drop to 3-4 on mobile), resolution={256} (NOT fullscreen — the transmission buffer is the #1 perf cost; cap it), backside={false}. Tint with color/attenuationColor toward #6CBAD9.

Inner blue plasma core: a smaller sphere (~0.55 radius) with an emissive material (meshBasicMaterial or a custom shader) using color #6CBAD9 / #BFE0EE, emissive lifted above 1.0 and toneMapped={false} so it drives selective Bloom and glows THROUGH the refractive shell. Animate emissiveIntensity with a slow sin() pulse in useFrame for a 'breathing' plasma feel.

Plasma displacement / liquid look: drive the inner core (or a mid-shell) with a noise-displaced custom ShaderMaterial (simplex/curl noise in the vertex shader, uTime uniform) OR rely on MeshTransmissionMaterial's distortion/temporalDistortion for a cheaper swirling-glass effect. Cheaper path preferred for perf; reserve custom noise shader for desktop high-tier.

Fresnel rim light: a thin shell or onBeforeCompile/ShaderMaterial computing pow(1.0 - dot(viewDir, normal), power) and mixing toward #BFE0EE — gives the bright edge halo that reads as 'magic energy' and separates the orb from the background.

Orbiting sparkles: drei <Sparkles count={40} scale={2.2} size={3} speed={0.4} noise={1.5} color='#BFE0EE'/> wrapped so they orbit the orb; toneMapped={false} on their material path so Bloom catches them as fairy-dust glints. Reduce count to ~15 on mobile.
Idle motion: drei <Float speed={1.2} rotationIntensity={0.4} floatIntensity={0.6}> around the orb group for a gentle conjured-levitation bob; disabled entirely under prefers-reduced-motion.

Realistic refraction: drei <Environment preset='city' /> (or a small custom HDR / blur={1}) so the glass has something to refract/reflect; use a lightweight preset or a <Lightformer>-built environment to avoid loading a big HDR. Environment with background={false}.

Soft bloom glow: <EffectComposer> + <Bloom mipmapBlur intensity={0.9} luminanceThreshold={0.6} luminanceSmoothing={0.3} kernelSize={KernelSize.LARGE}/> from @react-three/postprocessing. Selective by HDR — only the emissive core + sparkles (toneMapped=false, color>1) bloom, keeping the glass crisp. Disable EffectComposer on low-power tier.

Mouse-reactive light + parallax: a <pointLight> (color #6CBAD9) whose position lerps toward the normalized pointer each frame, plus a subtle group rotation lerp toward the pointer — gives the orb a living, light-following highlight and 3D parallax tilt. Listen on window pointermove (passive) and store normalized x/y in a ref; lerp in useFrame and invalidate().

On-demand perf control: <Canvas frameloop='demand'> — call invalidate() inside useFrame only while the orb is visible/interacting so it animates smoothly but idles to 0fps when offscreen/static, protecting battery (directly addresses the documented MeshTransmissionMaterial GPU-pegging issue).

Tier-aware quality: detect GPU/mobile (navigator.hardwareConcurrency, devicemicro, drei's useDetectGPU/PerformanceMonitor) and scale samples, transmission resolution, sparkle count, and whether Bloom/Environment mount.
orb.fallbackStrategy: Detection runs once on the client in the <MagicOrb> wrapper before deciding what to mount: (1) prefers-reduced-motion via window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') AND framer-motion's useReducedMotion(); (2) WebGL availability by attempting to get a 'webgl2' || 'webgl' context from a throwaway canvas inside a try/catch (also catches context-creation failures and blocked WebGL); (3) low-power / save-data via navigator.connection?.saveData, navigator.deviceMemory < 4, navigator.hardwareConcurrency <= 4, or a coarse-pointer + small-viewport heuristic for old phones; optionally drei's useDetectGPU tier===0. If reduced-motion is set, render a STATIC version of the fallback (no animation). If WebGL is missing OR low-power, render the animated CSS/canvas plasma-aura fallback instead of the Canvas. The fallback is a self-contained <OrbFallback> component: a layered pure-CSS orb — a radial-gradient sphere (from #BFE0EE highlight to #6CBAD9 to a deep navy edge), an inner conic/radial 'plasma' layer animated with @keyframes rotate + hue/scale breathing, a blurred ::before halo for the bloom feel, and a couple of small blurred white-blue dots as sparkles drifting via transform keyframes. For a richer fallback, optionally a 2D <canvas> drawing a few additive radial-gradient blobs swirling (requestAnimationFrame, paused when offscreen via IntersectionObserver, paused under reduced-motion). The fallback occupies the exact same box/size as the WebGL orb so layout is identical, and uses aria-hidden='true' since it's decorative. Both paths are wrapped in next/dynamic with ssr:false so three.js never runs on the server and there's no hydration mismatch; a plain CSS gradient placeholder shows during the dynamic import.
orb.perfAndA11y: DPR clamped: <Canvas dpr={[1, 1.75]}> (cap at ~1.75 even on retina — transmission + bloom at dpr 2 is the perf killer). frameloop='demand' with invalidate() only while visible/animating. gl={{ powerPreference:'high-performance', antialias:false, alpha:true, stencil:false, depth:true }} and performance={{ min:0.5 }} so r3f auto-degrades dpr under load. IntersectionObserver gates the Canvas: unmount or freeze (stop calling invalidate) when the orb scrolls offscreen so a static hero orb costs ~0fps while reading the rest of the page; also pause on document 'visibilitychange' (tab hidden). Cap MeshTransmissionMaterial resolution to 256 (the buffer it renders every frame) and samples to 6 desktop / 3-4 mobile — these two props dominate cost. Mount EffectComposer/Bloom and Environment only on capable tiers. next/dynamic ssr:false keeps three out of the SSR bundle and lazy-loads it (saves first-load JS; the heavy r3f/three chunk loads after hydration). Pointermove listener is passive and only sets a ref (no React re-render) — it never blocks scroll; the Canvas has pointer-events:none / touch-action:none where it overlays content so it never steals scroll or taps on mobile. Accessibility: orb is purely decorative — aria-hidden='true', role removed from canvas, no focus trap, and it must not convey information not available in text. prefers-reduced-motion fully honored (static fallback, Float/sparkle/pulse animations disabled). Respect Lenis smooth-scroll already in the stack by keeping the orb's pointer handling on window, not intercepting wheel. Provide a poster/static gradient first paint so there's never a blank box during import.
orb.perPageVariations: Treat it as ONE continuous, persistent orb whose "mood" shifts per route rather than six different orbs — so it reads as the genie companion following the visitor through the site. Implementation: keep a single shared orb state (Zustand store or React context mounted once in the root layout) holding the current variant + a target color/scale/energy, and animate (lerp) between targets on route change instead of remounting, optionally placing the <Canvas> in the persistent layout so the WebGL context survives navigation (App Router persistent layout) for a true continuous element. Per-page variants (driven by ORB_VARIANTS above): HOME — largest, centered hero, max energy: high distortion, full sparkles, strongest bloom, prominent mouse parallax; the conjured 'genie core'. ABOUT/Meet Us — calmer and smaller, slower float, softer pulse, sits to one side beside narrative copy; feels reflective/trustworthy. SERVICES — orb subtly splits its inner energy into 3 brighter plasma wisps / hue accents echoing the 3 pillars (or 3 orbiting sparkle clusters), more active distortion as it 'works'. PORTFOLIO — shrinks to a compact corner companion / cursor-trailing accent so case-study visuals lead; low sparkle count, holds bloom for sparkle. BLOG (Genie Tips) — smallest and quietest: a small fixed corner orb, bloom OFF, low samples for cheap always-on presence beside long-form reading; can act as a scroll-progress indicator (energy/pulse rises as you near article end). CONTACT — most playful/inviting: fastest float, highest sparkle count and distortion, brightest pulse, light leans strongest toward the cursor as if reaching toward the visitor to 'grant the wish' (book a call). Optional micro-interactions reused everywhere: orb brightens + sparkle burst on CTA hover ('Book a strategy call'), and a quick scale-pop + ripple on click. All variations are parameter changes to the same component (color stays anchored to #6CBAD9 / #BFE0EE on every page), never structural forks, so the magic device stays unmistakably one entity.

ds.themeConcept:
Name: "Conjured Clarity" — a deep night-sky-meets-clinical-clean aesthetic where the brand's blue genie orb is the only source of magic in an otherwise calm, trustworthy space.

The world is a deep midnight-indigo canvas (so the orb and sparkles can truly glow) that lifts into luminous sky-blue auroras around hero moments, then resolves into airy, near-white "clinical light" sections for proof, services, and reading-heavy pages. The genie does the magic; the medical professionalism keeps it grounded. Every page alternates between two registers: "conjuring" (dark, glowing, orb-led, glassmorphic) and "clarity" (light, spacious, high-contrast, evidence-led) — so the site feels magical at the seams and clinically credible in the substance. The brand blue #6CBAD9 is the protagonist in both registers: it is the orb's plasma, the link color, the glow, and the focus ring.
ds.colors:
ANCHOR (brand, non-negotiable):
- genie-500 #6CBAD9 (primary brand blue — orb core, links, primary glow)
- genie-200 #BFE0EE (light tint — soft fills, hover wash, sparkle haze)

BRAND BLUE RAMP (built around #6CBAD9, hue-consistent):
- genie-50  #F0F8FC (faintest wash / tinted section bg on light)
- genie-100 #DCEFF7
- genie-200 #BFE0EE  (verified light tint)
- genie-300 #9BD0E6
- genie-400 #82C5DF
- genie-500 #6CBAD9  (verified primary)
- genie-600 #4A9FC4  (interactive hover, AA text on light ≥ 4.5 at 18px+)
- genie-700 #2E7FA3  (link text on white, AA at body size — contrast ≈ 4.6:1)
- genie-800 #1E5C78
- genie-900 #143F54

DEEP / NIGHT-SKY BACKGROUNDS (the dark register):
- night-950 #070B1A  (deepest canvas / footer)
- night-900 #0B1226  (primary dark page bg)
- night-850 #0F1730  (raised dark surface)
- night-800 #16203F  (dark card base)
- night-700 #1E2B52  (dark card border / hairline)
Tip: these are indigo-leaning (slight violet) so the sky-blue orb reads as warm light against cool dark.

ACCENT / SPARKLE (used sparingly — the "stardust"):
- spark-cyan  #7FE9F0  (electric cyan highlight inside orb, link underline shimmer)
- spark-violet #8E7BE8 (aurora secondary, gradient companion to blue)
- spark-gold  #FFD79A (warm sparkle dots ONLY — 1–3 accents max per view, never UI structure)
Gold is the single "warm" note that signals trust/premium and keeps the palette from feeling cold/sterile.

TEXT:
On dark: text-onDark #EAF2F8 (primary), text-onDark-muted #A9BBD0 (secondary, ≈ 7:1 on night-900), text-onDark-faint #6E80A0 (captions only).
On light: ink-900 #0C1B2A (headlines — the near-navy from the hero), ink-700 #2A3A4C (body, ≈ 11:1 on white), ink-500 #5A6B7D (muted, ≈ 5:1 — AA body).
Never put genie-500 text on white for body copy (fails AA); use genie-700 for links/text and reserve genie-500 for ≥24px display or on-dark.

FEEDBACK:
- success #3FB984  - warning #E2A53A  - error #E5614B  - info = genie-600
(all tuned to sit in-palette, not jarring against the blue world)

SURFACES (light register):
- canvas-light #FFFFFF, surface-tint #F0F8FC (genie-50), hairline-light #E2EDF3

GRADIENT RECIPES (the magic):
1. "Aurora Hero" (dark hero bg): radial-gradient(120% 120% at 50% 0%, #16203F 0%, #0B1226 55%, #070B1A 100%) with an overlaid radial bloom behind the orb: radial-gradient(closest-side, rgba(108,186,217,.55), rgba(108,186,217,0) 70%).
2. "Conjure Sweep" (the bright sky-blue field from the drafts): linear-gradient(135deg, #6CBAD9 0%, #82C5DF 28%, #8E7BE8 72%, #7FE9F0 100%) — used on bright CTA bands and light-hero variants.
3. "Plasma Orb Core" (the orb itself): radial-gradient(circle at 35% 30%, #EAF8FE 0%, #7FE9F0 18%, #6CBAD9 45%, #2E7FA3 78%, #143F54 100%).
4. "Stardust Veil" (subtle texture over dark): radial sparkle field of genie-200 / spark-gold dots at 4–10% opacity.
5. "Genie Text" (gradient headline accent on one keyword only): linear-gradient(92deg, #6CBAD9, #8E7BE8 60%, #7FE9F0).
6. "Clarity Fade" (section transition dark→light): linear-gradient(180deg, #0B1226 0%, #0F1730 40%, #F0F8FC 100%).
ds.typography:
Pairing (Google Fonts):
- Display / Headings: "Sora" — geometric, slightly futuristic humanist sans. Feels modern, optimistic and a touch magical without being gimmicky; pairs perfectly with the orb's clean geometry. Weights 600/700 for H1–H2, 600 for H3.
- Body / UI: "Inter" — the trust workhorse: superb legibility at small sizes, neutral and clinical-credible. Weights 400 (body), 500 (UI labels, nav), 600 (emphasis).
- Optional mono accent: "JetBrains Mono" 500 for data/metrics, kicker labels ("RESEARCH. DATA. RESULTS."), and SERP/keyword chips — reinforces the "data" pillar.

Rationale: Sora supplies personality (the genie/wonder), Inter supplies trust (healthcare credibility). Two families max keeps it premium and fast.

Type scale (1.250 major-third, fluid via clamp):
- Display/H1: clamp(2.5rem, 1.6rem + 4.2vw, 4.5rem) / line-height 1.05 / weight 700 / letter-spacing -0.02em (Sora)
- H2: clamp(2rem, 1.4rem + 2.6vw, 3rem) / 1.12 / 700 / -0.015em
- H3: clamp(1.375rem, 1.1rem + 1.1vw, 1.75rem) / 1.2 / 600 / -0.01em
- H4: 1.25rem / 1.3 / 600
- Lead/intro: 1.25rem / 1.6 / 400 (Inter), color muted
- Body: 1.0625rem (17px) / 1.7 / 400 — generous for clinical reading
- Small/caption: 0.875rem / 1.5 / 400
- Kicker/eyebrow: 0.75rem / 1.4 / 600 / uppercase / letter-spacing 0.18em (Inter or JetBrains Mono)
- Button label: 0.9375rem / 1 / 600 / letter-spacing 0.02em

Usage rules:
- Apply the "Genie Text" gradient to ONE keyword per headline max (e.g. "searching", "trust") — mirrors the revamp drafts; everything else stays solid for legibility.
- Headlines on dark = text-onDark #EAF2F8; on light = ink-900 #0C1B2A.
- Body line length capped at ~68ch for reading pages (Genie Tips/blog).
- Never set body copy below 16px; never use Sora below H4 size.
ds.spacingRadii:
Spacing scale (4px base, Tailwind-aligned, with magical-airy additions):
0, 1=4, 2=8, 3=12, 4=16, 5=20, 6=24, 8=32, 10=40, 12=48, 16=64, 20=80, 24=96, 32=128, 40=160, 48=192.
Custom keys to extend: section=96px (mobile) / section-lg=160px (desktop) vertical rhythm between sections; gutter=24px mobile / 40px desktop. Generous whitespace is a brand requirement — default section padding py-24 md:py-40.

Border radii (soft, friendly, premium — nothing sharp):
- xs 6px (chips, tags, inputs-small)
- sm 10px (inputs, small buttons)
- md 14px (buttons, nav pills)
- lg 20px (cards)
- xl 28px (feature cards, modals)
- 2xl 36px (hero glass panels, large media)
- pill 999px (CTA pills like the drafts, badges, the orb halo ring)
- orb 50% (the orb + circular brand-core motifs)
Default card radius = lg(20px); hero glass panels = 2xl(36px); all primary CTAs = pill.

Container widths:
- content max-w 1200px (--container: 75rem) standard pages
- prose max-w 720px (reading / blog)
- wide max-w 1320px (portfolio grids, full-bleed hero inner)
- Page side padding: clamp(1.25rem, 5vw, 2.5rem).

Grid: 12-col, gap 24px (mobile) → 40px (desktop). Service pillars = 3-col on desktop, 1-col stacked mobile. Sparkle/orb elements may overflow containers intentionally (overflow-visible) for the conjured/floating feel.
ds.glowsShadows:
Two shadow families: "Lift" (real elevation, used on light register for trust/clarity) and "Glow" (the magical aura, used on dark register and interactive states).

LIFT (neutral, soft, low-contrast — premium not heavy), tuned for the blue world (shadows carry a faint indigo tint, not pure black):
- shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(12,27,42,0.06)
- shadow-sm: 0 2px 8px rgba(12,27,42,0.06), 0 1px 2px rgba(12,27,42,0.04)
- shadow-md: 0 8px 24px rgba(20,32,64,0.10), 0 2px 6px rgba(20,32,64,0.06)
- shadow-lg: 0 20px 50px rgba(20,32,64,0.14), 0 6px 14px rgba(20,32,64,0.08)
- shadow-card: 0 12px 32px rgba(30,92,120,0.12) — slight genie tint for branded cards on light

GLOW (the magical aura — brand blue light bleeding outward):
- glow-orb (the big halo around the hero orb): 0 0 80px 12px rgba(108,186,217,0.55), 0 0 160px 40px rgba(108,186,217,0.30), 0 0 240px 80px rgba(142,123,232,0.18)
- glow-sm: 0 0 16px rgba(108,186,217,0.45)
- glow-md: 0 0 28px rgba(108,186,217,0.5), 0 0 56px rgba(108,186,217,0.25)
- glow-cta (primary button resting): 0 8px 24px rgba(46,127,163,0.35), 0 0 0 1px rgba(108,186,217,0.4) inset
- glow-cta-hover: 0 10px 30px rgba(46,127,163,0.45), 0 0 36px rgba(108,186,217,0.55)
- glow-cyan: 0 0 24px rgba(127,233,240,0.5) — for spark/cyan highlights only
- glow-violet: 0 0 40px rgba(142,123,232,0.4) — aurora secondary accents

INNER HIGHLIGHTS (give glass/orb dimensionality):
- inner-top: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.25) — top rim light on glass cards (dark register)
- inner-orb: inset 0 0 60px rgba(255,255,255,0.35) — interior bloom of the orb

FOCUS RING (accessibility, on-brand): 0 0 0 3px rgba(108,186,217,0.55), 0 0 0 5px rgba(11,18,38,0.9) on dark / 0 0 0 3px rgba(46,127,163,0.5) on light. Always visible, never removed.

Usage: Glow is reserved for the orb, primary CTAs, focus, and at most one accent per section — overuse kills the magic. Light/clarity sections use Lift only. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by keeping glows static (no pulsing).
ds.glassmorphism:
Two glass tiers, register-specific. Always pair backdrop-blur with a semi-opaque fallback color (so it degrades gracefully where backdrop-filter is unsupported) and a 1px hairline border + top inner highlight for the "rim of light" read.

GLASS ON DARK (default — hero panels, floating nav, orb-companion tooltips, CTA pills like the drafts):
- background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06) layered over a fallback of rgba(22,32,63,0.6) (night-800 @60%)
- backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(140%)
- border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14)
- box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.22) (top rim) + 0 16px 40px rgba(7,11,26,0.5) (drop)
- optional brand tint variant: swap base to rgba(108,186,217,0.10) for genie-tinted glass (active nav, selected chip)

GLASS ON LIGHT (proof cards, sticky CTA bar over light sections):
- background: rgba(255,255,255,0.7)
- backdrop-filter: blur(16px) saturate(120%)
- border: 1px solid rgba(226,237,243,0.9) (hairline-light)
- box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(20,32,64,0.08), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.8)

FROSTED PILL (the hero CTAs from the drafts): glass-on-dark recipe + border-radius pill + glow-cta on the primary; secondary CTA = same glass with no glow, border rgba(255,255,255,0.22).

Rules: max blur 20px (perf + legibility); never put long body copy on glass — glass is for CTAs, nav, badges, stat cards, and short labels. Ensure text on glass meets AA against the BLURRED-content worst case (assume the lighter background behind) — keep glass text at #EAF2F8 on dark glass. Provide a no-backdrop-filter fallback class that bumps the base fill opacity to ~0.85.
ds.motionPrinciples:
Motion language: "The genie conjures, then settles." Elements arrive as if summoned — a soft rise + fade + faint glow bloom — then hold perfectly still and legible. Magic is in the entrance and in the always-alive orb; UI chrome stays calm.

EASING:
- ease-genie (signature, for entrances/reveals): cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) — confident overshoot-free "settle"
- ease-out-soft (hovers, micro): cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)
- ease-float (orb idle, ambient): cubic-bezier(0.45, 0, 0.55, 1) (sinusoidal feel)
- ease-standard (color/opacity): cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)

DURATIONS:
- micro (hover, focus, button press): 150–200ms
- ui (cards, accordions, nav): 250–350ms
- reveal (section entrance): 600–800ms
- orb idle loops: 6–10s (slow bob + plasma swirl), sparkle twinkle 2–4s offset/random
- page transition: 400ms crossfade + 8px rise

ENTRANCE REVEALS (scroll): translateY(16–24px) + opacity 0→1 + optional blur(6px)→0, staggered 60–90ms per item, triggered once at ~15% in-view (IntersectionObserver). Headlines reveal word/line; the gradient keyword does a 700ms shimmer sweep once on first view.

SCROLL BEHAVIOR: gentle parallax on the orb and stardust only (max 20–40px travel) — never on text. Sticky glass nav condenses (height + blur) after 80px. No scroll-jacking. Smooth-scroll for anchor links.

THE ORB (centerpiece): always-alive ambient state = slow vertical bob (±8px, 7s), internal plasma swirl, periodic sparkle emission. On cursor proximity it leans/parallaxes toward the pointer and brightens (glow-md→glow-orb). On hover/click of CTAs it can pulse once. WebGL via react-three-fiber; CSS/canvas plasma-aura fallback uses layered radial gradients + slow rotate + opacity breathing.

SHOULD animate: orb, sparkles, section entrances, CTA hover/glow, nav condense, keyword gradient shimmer, number count-ups on stats.
SHOULD NOT animate: body paragraphs, form fields while typing, anything that delays reading or reflows layout, no infinite pulsing on text or large surfaces.

ACCESSIBILITY (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) — REQUIRED: disable orb bob/swirl/parallax/sparkles (render a static glowing orb image/gradient), replace all entrance transforms with instant opacity (or no) change, stop the keyword shimmer, freeze glows (no pulsing), keep only essential <=120ms color/opacity feedback. Motion is decorative — the site must be fully usable and beautiful with zero animation.

ia.siteMap:
CLINIC GENIE — MULTI-PAGE SITE MAP (Next.js App Router routes)

/ (Home)
  Purpose: Convert clinic owners and specialist doctors in one scroll. Lead with the new hero promise, prove credibility fast (results, healthcare-specific positioning, compliance-awareness), preview the 3 service pillars and 3-step process, surface portfolio proof and partners, and drive to "Book a strategy call." The orb is the persistent hero companion that follows the scroll.

/about (About Us)
  Purpose: Establish trust and the "genie" origin story — who Clinic Genie is, why a healthcare-only agency exists, the values (Research. Data. Results.), compliance philosophy, and the people. Answers "can I trust you with my clinic's reputation?"

/services (Services)
  Purpose: The full offer. Deep-dive the 3 pillars (Business Strategy & Development, Digital Growth Solutions, Brand & Experience Design) with sub-services, the 3-step process explained, and how engagements work. Internal anchor links: /services#strategy, /services#growth, /services#brand, /services#process.

/portfolio (Portfolio / Our Works)
  Purpose: Proof through case studies. Filterable grid of clinic projects with outcomes; each opens a detail view. Drives "See our works" CTA from Home.
  Child route: /portfolio/[slug] — individual case study detail pages (e.g. /portfolio/the-acne-clinic, /portfolio/the-aesthetics-clinic, /portfolio/stellaris-fertility, /portfolio/orchard-orthopaedics, /portfolio/lumiere-dental).

/genie-tips (Genie Tips — blog index)
  Purpose: SEO content hub + thought leadership for clinic owners. Featured post, category filters (SEO, SEM & Ads, AI Search, Compliance, Web & Brand, Growth Strategy), searchable post grid, newsletter capture.
  Child route: /genie-tips/[slug] — individual blog post pages.

/meet-us (Meet Us)
  Purpose: The team + culture page (kept from current nav). Faces, roles, the "rub the lamp" booking nudge. Can be merged into /about as a section, but retained as a distinct route to honour existing nav and SEO.

/contact (Contact)

- One persistent, recurring orb component (react-three-fiber/three.js) themed brand blue #6CBAD9 with #BFE0EE highlights, wrapped in the orbiting genie-lamp "G" ring + sparkle from the logo/revamp comp.
- States: idle breathing glow · cursor-attracted lean (hero/contact) · guide-light travel (process steps) · charge-up (stats/results) · satellite orblets (partners) · celebratory burst (CTA/submit).
- Graceful degradation: NON-WEBGL fallback = CSS/canvas radial-gradient plasma aura that pulses; prefers-reduced-motion = static glowing orb with no parallax, count-ups, or bursts. Always present, never blocking content or harming accessibility/contrast.

ix.orbAsCompanion:
THE ORB AS PERSISTENT COMPANION (single instance, never re-mounted)

Architecture: Mount ONE <OrbCompanion/> in app/layout.tsx, OUTSIDE the route slot, so it survives App Router page transitions. It lives in a fixed full-viewport <div className="pointer-events-none fixed inset-0 z-30">. The actual orb is positioned by Framer Motion `transform`, not layout, so it can fly anywhere. All page content reserves a transparent "orb anchor" via an empty div with `data-orb-anchor="hero|services|..."` and a stable id; the orb reads anchor rects and animates toward them. This is the spine of the whole site — the orb is a global singleton that pages talk to, not a per-page decoration.

State: a tiny Zustand-free React context `OrbProvider` holding { mood, target, scale, intensity, isThinking }. Moods: 'idle' | 'curious' | 'thinking' | 'celebrate' | 'focus'. Each page sets mood via a `useOrbScene({mood, anchor})` hook in a useEffect on mount and on scroll milestones. The R3F orb maps mood -> shader uniforms (uTurbulence, uColorMix between #6CBAD9 core and #BFE0EE rim, uPulseSpeed) tweened with `MathUtils.damp` for buttery transitions — never hard cuts.

Cross-page travel: On route change, Framer Motion's AnimatePresence wraps page content for fade/slide, but the orb does NOT exit. Instead it runs a "transit" tween: shrink to ~0.6 scale, drift to the new page's primary anchor with a slight parabolic arc (animate x/y with a custom ease and a midpoint via keyframes), trailing a particle wake, then settle and bloom back to full scale as new content reveals. Feels like the genie carries you between rooms.

Anchor handoff detail: useOrbScene registers the current page's anchor sequence. A single `requestAnimationFrame` loop (one rAF for the whole app, shared) reads the active anchor's getBoundingClientRect each frame and damps the orb's spring target toward anchor center. Because it's rAF + spring, scrolling, resizing, and layout shifts all keep the orb glued without jank.

Performance guardrails: the R3F <Canvas frameloop="demand"> only re-renders when uniforms or camera change (invalidate() on scroll/mood change). One PointLight, one icosahedron with a custom MeshDistortMaterial-style shader, max ~2k particle wake via instanced points. dpr={[1, 2]} capped. On mobile, particle count and distortion drop by 60%.

Fallback chain: detect WebGL via a 1-time canvas.getContext('webgl2')||'webgl' probe in OrbProvider. If absent -> render <OrbCanvas2D/> (a Canvas2D radial-gradient plasma with 2 drifting noise blobs) OR pure-CSS aura (stacked radial-gradients + blur + CSS @keyframes breathing). The companion API (mood/target) is identical, so pages don't know which renderer is active. If prefers-reduced-motion: render a static CSS orb that only does a gentle 4s opacity breathe, no travel, no parallax — it still moves to anchors but via instant `layout` jumps with crossfade, not arcs.
ix.scrollytelling:
SCROLL-DRIVEN STORYTELLING (Lenis is already installed — use it as the scroll source of truth)

Setup: Wrap the app in a Lenis smooth-scroll provider (lerp ~0.1). Bridge Lenis to Framer Motion: on Lenis 'scroll', call a MotionValue `scrollY.set(e.scroll)`, then derive per-section progress with `useScroll({ target: ref, offset: ['start end','end start'] })`. Drive the orb shader's uScroll uniform from the same MotionValue so 3D and DOM stay frame-locked. Respect reduced-motion by disabling Lenis (native scroll) and collapsing all scroll-tied transforms to simple opacity fades.

HOMEPAGE BEAT MAP (the orb is the protagonist; each beat morphs it):

Beat 0 — Hero / "Conjuring" (0–100vh): Orb materializes from a dim spark to full plasma over the first 600ms (entrance), then sits center-right, large, slow breathe, mood='idle'. Hero copy "The right patients are searching. Make sure your clinic is the one they trust." reveals word-by-word as the orb's first pulse. As you begin scrolling, the orb shrinks slightly and the medical cross from the logo glints inside it once.

Beat 1 — "The search" (100–200vh): mood='curious'. Faint search-query phrases ("knee specialist near me", "best dermatologist Singapore") drift INTO the orb as particles and get absorbed — visualizing patient intent. Orb tints brighter cyan (#6CBAD9 -> push toward #BFE0EE rim) as it "ingests" demand. Implement as DOM text spans whose x/y animate toward the orb anchor on scroll progress, fading at 80% of the path; matching particles spawn in R3F.

Beat 2 — "Three wishes" / Services reveal (200–340vh): mood='focus'. Orb travels left and contracts; three service "wish" cards emerge as if exhaled from it, each card sliding in with a staggered spring while a thin light-thread connects orb->card (animated SVG stroke-dashoffset). Pinned mini-section: the orb stays sticky while the three pillars scroll past, each pillar lighting its own thread as it enters viewport.

Beat 3 — "The process" (340–460vh): Orb morphs into a 3-node path. Strategy & Research -> Implementation & Design -> Results & Optimisation render as 3 waypoints; a glowing line draws between them tied to scroll progress (pathLength MotionValue). The orb slides along the line as you scroll, parking at each node and pulsing once when that node's text reveals.

Beat 4 — "Results" (460–560vh): mood='celebrate'. Stat counters (e.g., ranking lifts, leads) count up via animated MotionValues triggered on whileInView. Orb does a brighter bloom + brief particle burst at the moment the headline stat crosses its target.

Beat 5 — "Trust / compliance" (560–640vh): mood='focus', orb cools to calmer steady glow (lower turbulence) signaling premium/compliance-aware seriousness — a deliberate contrast that reassures doctors. Partner logos (Real Inbound, Qianyi, Inteq, Pilot Pulse) fade up in a quiet grid.

Beat 6 — CTA / "Make your wish" (640–700vh): Orb returns to center and enlarges, "Book a strategy call" button gains a magnetic pull + the orb leans toward the cursor. This is the conversion crescendo.

Mechanics: each beat = one <Section> with a ref; a `useBeat(progress)` hook maps the section's 0..1 progress to orb uniforms and DOM transforms. Use sticky/pin via CSS `position: sticky` + tall spacer sections (no scroll-jacking libraries needed beyond Lenis). Keep transform-only animations (translate/scale/opacity) for 60fps.
ix.cursorEffects:
CURSOR & POINTER REACTIONS

1) Orb follows light, not the cursor (premium subtlety): The cursor acts as a moving light source. Track pointer in a global MotionValue {mx,my} (normalized -1..1). Feed it to the R3F PointLight position (parallaxed, clamped) so the orb's specular highlight and rim glow shift toward the cursor — the orb appears to "look at" the user. Damp with `MathUtils.damp` (factor ~4) so it glides, never snaps. On idle (no pointer move >3s) the highlight drifts in a slow Lissajous figure so it never looks dead.

2) Custom cursor (the genie wisp): Replace the default cursor with a two-part custom cursor rendered in a fixed pointer-events-none layer:
   - A small solid dot (8px, #6CBAD9) that tracks pointer 1:1 (instant).
   - A larger trailing ring (28px, #BFE0EE, 40% opacity) that follows via spring (stiffness 150, damping 20) — classic lag-trail.
   Hover states change the cursor contextually: over links/buttons the ring scales to 1.6 and label-magnetizes; over the orb the ring becomes a faint sparkle cluster; over draggable/interactive constellation nodes it shows a tiny "grab" wisp. Implement with a single component subscribing to pointermove + a Framer `useSpring`. Hide on touch devices (pointer: coarse) and when prefers-reduced-motion (restore native cursor).

3) Pointer ripples on click: Each click spawns a short-lived expanding ring at the click point (a Framer motion.div keyed by an incrementing id, scale 0->3, opacity 1->0 over 500ms, then unmount). On the orb itself, a click sends a ripple THROUGH the shader (uClickRipple uniform animated 0->1 with a decay) — the plasma visibly reacts to touch. Caps at a small pool of ripples to avoid DOM churn.

4) Pointer-driven parallax background: Faint star/dust field (CSS or a low-cost canvas) shifts opposite the cursor (translate by mx*8px, my*8px) for depth. Disabled under reduced-motion.

5) Device tilt on mobile: Use deviceorientation (with iOS permission gate) to drive the same {mx,my} light position so the orb reacts to phone tilt — graceful no-op if permission denied. This keeps the "alive" feeling without a mouse.

All pointer math runs through ONE shared listener writing to MotionValues; components subscribe. No per-component window listeners — keeps it cheap and consistent.
ix.microInteractions:
MICRO-INTERACTIONS (consistent magical grammar; one shared easing + token set)

Motion tokens (define once in a `motion.ts`): ease.glide = [0.22,1,0.36,1] (expo-out feel), ease.spring = {type:'spring', stiffness:260, damping:24}, dur.fast=0.18, dur.base=0.4, dur.slow=0.8. Color pulses always travel #6CBAD9 -> #BFE0EE. Everything reuses these so the whole site feels authored by one hand.

BUTTONS (magnetic + conjure): Primary CTAs ("Book a strategy call", "See our works") are magnetic — on pointermove within ~80px, translate the button toward the cursor by (dist*0.25) and the label by (dist*0.4) for a parallax pull, springing back on leave (the well-known magnetic-button technique via useSpring). On hover: a soft #BFE0EE glow blooms behind (box-shadow/blur layer animating in), the orb's mood ticks toward 'celebrate', and a 1px sheen sweeps across (translateX gradient). On click: brief 0.96 scale dip + a ripple emanating from click point. Secondary/ghost buttons: just the sheen + border-glow, no magnet (hierarchy).

CARDS (service & portfolio): On hover, lift with `translateY(-6px)` + scale 1.02 + shadow bloom in brand blue; the card's inner icon does a tiny 6° tilt toward cursor (3D rotateX/rotateY from cursor offset, max 8°, perspective 800px). A faint orb-thread (SVG) brightens from the companion to the hovered card. On leave, spring back. Cards reveal on scroll with stagger (parent `staggerChildren: 0.08`, child y:24->0 + opacity).

NAV: Sticky top nav (About Us, Portfolio, Genie Tips, Meet Us, Contact). Active link has an animated underline using `layoutId="nav-underline"` so it slides between items (shared layout animation). On hover, link text gets a quick char-stagger lift. Mobile menu opens as a full-screen "lamp smoke" reveal: a clip-path circle expands from the menu button; nav items fade-up staggered; the orb floats behind the menu, enlarged, mood='curious'.

LINKS / TEXT: Inline links get an animated gradient underline (background-size 0%->100% on hover). Section headings reveal with a mask-up (clip-path inset or overflow-hidden + y translate) on whileInView, once:true.

FORMS (Contact): Inputs glow at the focused field's border (#6CBAD9), label floats up, and the orb turns mood='thinking' while typing (subtle faster pulse) and 'celebrate' on successful submit with a particle burst + checkmark draw (SVG pathLength 0->1). Validation errors give a 2px horizontal shake (x:[-4,4,-4,0], 0.25s) — never jarring.

REVEALS (global): A single `<Reveal>` wrapper component (variants: up/scale/mask) used everywhere for consistency; uses `useInView({once:true, margin:'-10% 0px'})`. Numbers/stats use a `<CountUp>` MotionValue. Under reduced-motion, all reveals collapse to a 200ms opacity fade with zero translate, and magnetic/tilt/parallax are fully disabled.
ix.perPageInteractions:
SIGNATURE INTERACTION PER PAGE (each page gives the orb a distinct verb)

HOME — "Make a wish" orb prompt: Below the hero, an inline input "Tell the orb what your clinic needs…" with chips (More patients · Rank #1 on Google · A new website · Trust & compliance). On submit/chip-click, the orb pulses mood='thinking', "inhales" the text as particles, then "exhales" a tailored 1-line response + a deep link to the matching service. No backend needed: a small keyword->response map client-side (and chips are deterministic). It's a delightful router that turns the central metaphor into a real CTA. Reduced-motion: instant text swap, no particles.

ABOUT US — Orb as narrator/scroll-spy: The orb docks to the side and acts as a progress indicator down the story (founding -> mission -> healthcare focus). As each paragraph enters view, the orb emits a small spark and the paragraph's first letter illuminates. A vertical "light thread" timeline draws via scroll-tied pathLength connecting milestones.

SERVICES — Three wishes orbiting: The three pillars (Business Strategy & Development, Digital Growth Solutions, Brand & Experience Design) render as orbs ORBITING the central companion in a slow ring (R3F: three small instanced orbs on a rotating group; DOM fallback: CSS @keyframes orbit with transform-origin). Hover a wish -> it breaks orbit, flies forward to center, and its sub-services fan out as labeled satellites (Market Research / Strategic Planning / Partnership & Grant Consultancy, etc.). Click -> routes to detail. Keyboard: arrow keys cycle which wish is "forward" (focus ring + aria-live announces it). Reduced-motion: static trefoil layout, hover just expands the sub-service list.

PORTFOLIO — Constellation gallery: Projects are stars in a 2D constellation on a deep-blue field. The companion orb is the "north star" you drag-pan from (or it auto-pans). Hovering a star draws connecting lines to related projects (same service category) and pops a thumbnail card; clicking zooms the camera into a case study (Framer layout/scale transition into the detail view). Filter chips (by service pillar) re-cluster the stars with a spring re-layout (FLIP via Framer `layout`). Reduced-motion / no-WebGL: a clean responsive grid with hover-lift cards and the same filter re-layout (still springy but transform-only).

GENIE TIPS (blog) — Orb librarian: Article cards float gently; the orb hovers near the search/filter and "sorts" — on tag select, cards re-flow with FLIP and the orb tints to the tag's accent. Featured post gets the orb parked beside it pulsing. Reading view: a thin top progress bar IS a stretched sliver of the orb's glow (reading progress tied to scroll).

MEET US — Orb introductions: Team photos in a row; hovering a person makes the orb drift to them and "introduce" (name/role reveal beside the orb, a soft spotlight gradient follows). Subtle: each portrait gets a duotone-to-color transition in brand blue on hover.

CONTACT — Grant a wish / send to the genie: The form is framed as making your wish. On submit, the orb gathers the field contents as particles, compresses to a bright point, and "sends" them (a particle stream flying up/off-screen) before resolving to a success state with the address (164 Bukit Merah Central #03-3625) and social links materializing. mood: thinking -> celebrate. Reduced-motion: standard form with a calm success fade.
ix.signatureMoments:
2–3 HERO "WOW" MOMENTS (the things people screenshot and remember)

WOW #1 — The Conjuring (hero entrance + logo unity): On first load, the screen is near-black with a single spark. The genie-lamp "G" swirl (from the logo, as an SVG path) draws itself via stroke-dashoffset (pathLength 0->1, ~1.1s) and spirals inward; at the moment the swirl completes, it "ignites" the orb — the R3F plasma blooms from the swirl's center, the medical cross glints once inside the core, and the hero copy reveals word-by-word. This literally animates the brand mark INTO the interactive companion, fusing logo and orb so the metaphor reads instantly. Implement: SVG overlay for the swirl (Framer motion path), a `onAnimationComplete` that flips OrbProvider to 'materialize' (shader uMaterialize 0->1 over 600ms via a tweened uniform), then triggers the hero text stagger. One-time per session (sessionStorage flag); returning users get a 400ms quick-bloom instead. Reduced-motion: static lit orb + simple fade-in of copy, no draw.

WOW #2 — Ask the Orb / "Make a wish" reveal (home signature): Detailed above — but as a moment, the magic is the inhale/exhale: typed intent visibly turns into particles that spiral INTO the orb (DOM letters tween to orb anchor, R3F absorbs matching particles, mood='thinking' with faster turbulent pulse + dimming), a held beat (~700ms "the genie considers"), then an exhale bloom that pushes out the answer card with a particle puff and mood='celebrate'. The orb feeling like it genuinely "thinks" is the emotional hook that separates this from a normal agency site. Implement: a small state machine (idle->inhaling->thinking->answering) driving both DOM and shader; deterministic client-side answer map keyed to chips + a few keywords so it's reliable and instant.

WOW #3 — Three Wishes orbit -> process morph (services/home transition): As you scroll from the services beat into the process beat, the three orbiting service-orbs converge and MERGE back into the central orb, which then stretches into the 3-node process path (Strategy & Research -> Implementation & Design -> Results & Optimisation) with the connecting light-line drawing on scroll. It's a single continuous metaphor — wishes become a method — achieved by sharing ONE orb group whose children's positions and the parent's shape are both scroll-progress driven (no unmount between sections). This continuity (nothing pops in/out; everything transforms) is what makes the site feel genuinely "alive" and premium rather than animated-for-the-sake-of-it. Implement: a pinned sticky stage spanning both beats; a single MotionValue `t` (0..1 across the pin) lerps orbit-radius->0 (converge), then drives node positions + SVG pathLength. Reduced-motion / no-WebGL: skip convergence; just reveal the 3 process steps as a clean stepped diagram with the line drawing on whileInView.

Why these three: #1 sells the brand fusion in 2 seconds, #2 makes the orb feel sentient and doubles as a conversion router, #3 proves the orb is one continuous companion (not scattered effects) — together they make Clinic Genie feel magical yet controlled, which is exactly the premium + trustworthy + compliance-aware tone specialist doctors need.

voice.voiceNotes:
CLINIC GENIE BRAND VOICE — "A wise genie for serious clinic owners."

THE PERSONA: Clinic Genie speaks like a calm, well-read advisor who happens to wield a little magic. Think a seasoned strategist who has seen the Singapore healthcare market for years, now distilled into a genie. Wonder comes from the imagery and the orb; credibility comes from specifics, restraint, and respect for the doctor's intelligence. The genie never oversells, never jokes about patient outcomes, and never promises miracles it cannot measure.

VOICE PILLARS:
1. Wonder, earned. We use one magical idea per moment (a wish, a conjure, an orb), then immediately ground it in a concrete benefit or proof. Magic is the frame; results are the substance.
2. Quiet confidence. Short, declarative sentences. We state outcomes plainly. We let the data carry the boast.
3. Clinical respect. Audience is specialist doctors and clinic owners. Address them as peers and decision-makers, never as patients. Assume they are busy, discerning, and compliance-conscious.
4. Singapore-fluent. Reference local realities naturally: MOH advertising guidelines (PHMC/HCSA), CHAS/Medisave-aware patients, Google SG and "near me" search, GST-registered clinics, the competitive Orchard/Novena/Mount Elizabeth landscape. Use British/Singapore spelling: optimisation, specialise, colour, programme, enquiry, centre.

DO:
- Open with the patient's search behaviour or the clinic owner's real problem, then offer the wish.
- Pair every magical verb (conjure, grant, summon, reveal) with a measurable noun (rankings, qualified enquiries, booked consults, page-one visibility).
- Name the discipline precisely: healthcare SEO, medical SEM, AI search visibility, compliance-aware copy. Specificity reads as competence.
- Be compliance-aware out loud where relevant — it is a feature, not a disclaimer. "Copy that persuades without crossing MOH guidelines."
- Use "your clinic", "your patients", "your practice" — make the doctor the hero; the genie is the helper.
- Keep numbers honest and conditional ("designed to", "built to", "so that") rather than guaranteed.

DON'T:
- No corny genie clichés: no "your wish is my command", no "abracadabra", no "poof", no excessive exclamation marks, no rubbing-the-lamp puns, no three-wishes gimmick used literally as a limit.
- Never promise specific patient health outcomes or cure/treatment claims — that is the clinic's clinical domain and a compliance risk.
- No hype adjectives stacked together ("amazing, incredible, revolutionary"). One vivid word beats three weak ones.
- Don't infantilise the reader or explain medicine to doctors. Explain marketing; respect their expertise.
- Avoid fear-based or scarcity manipulation ("your competitors are stealing patients!"). State the stakes calmly and factually.
- No emoji in body copy. Sparkle belongs in the visual orb, not the text.

MAGIC LEXICON (use sparingly, max one per block): conjure, grant, reveal, summon, illuminate, the orb, the wish, foresight, the genie's lens. AVOID: spell, hocus, magic words, wishes-remaining, lamp-rubbing.

MICRO-RULE: If a sentence would still work for a generic agency, add the magic; if a sentence is all magic and no proof, add the number. Every line should pass both tests.

voice.serviceWishes:
THE THREE WISHES — Clinic Genie grants three kinds of wishes. Each pillar is a wish; each sub-service is how the genie grants it.

═══════════════════════════════════════════
WISH ONE — "I wish my clinic had a clear path to grow."
PILLAR: Business Strategy & Development
Lead benefit: Before we touch a single ad or webpage, the genie's lens reveals where your clinic actually stands — and the shortest route to where it should be.

• Market Research — "Show me what my patients really want."
We map demand, competitors, and patient behaviour across your specialty in Singapore, so every decision rests on evidence, not guesswork.

• Strategic Planning — "Give me a plan I can act on Monday."
A focused growth roadmap with priorities, timelines, and targets — the kind a specialist clinic can actually execute between consultations.

• Partnership & Grant Consultancy — "Help me grow without burning cash."
We surface the right partners and government grants (yes, the EDG and PSG-eligible ones), so your growth is part-funded by schemes you're entitled to use.

═══════════════════════════════════════════
WISH TWO — "I wish the right patients could find me online."
PILLAR: Digital Growth Solutions
Lead benefit: This is where the orb does its most visible work — turning quiet search results into a steady stream of qualified enquiries.

• Healthcare SEO — "Put my clinic on page one."
We earn durable rankings for the conditions and treatments your ideal patients search — built to satisfy Google and MOH guidelines alike.

• Medical SEM — "Bring me patients now, not next quarter."
Precisely targeted search ads that capture high-intent patients the moment they're ready to book — every dollar tracked to a real enquiry.

• Copywriting — "Make my words persuade without crossing the line."
Compliance-aware copy that builds trust and converts, written for discerning patients and within Singapore's healthcare advertising rules.

• Lead Generation — "Fill my appointment book, not my spam folder."
Conversion-focused journeys and capture systems that turn visitors into booked consultations — qualified patients, not noise.

═══════════════════════════════════════════
WISH THREE — "I wish my brand looked as good as my care."
PILLAR: Brand & Experience Design
Lead benefit: The genie makes your clinic look — and feel — like the trusted specialist you already are, online and off.

• Branding & Logo Design — "Give my clinic an identity patients remember."
A distinctive, premium identity that signals expertise and earns trust before the first appointment.

• Web Design & Development — "Build me a website that works as hard as I do."
Fast, beautiful, mobile-first clinic websites engineered to convert and easy for your team to maintain.

• Photography & Video — "Show patients the people behind the practice."
Polished clinical photography and video that humanise your team and reassure patients they're in expert hands.
